commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainer review is needed because the fix changes low-level fs-safe semantics and needs an owner decision on cache scope, validation, and gateway freshness before merge.; Needs attention: The patch introduces a concrete fs-safe boundary concern because cached root JSON reads can bypass hardlink, boundary, size, and stat validation.; Review finding: [P1] Keep root-safe reads out of a path-only cache — src/infra/jso... (sha=84fb71f43ec821ffe425f3fc26daf218a287d81a)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a process-scoped sync JSON read cache in `src/infra/json-files.ts` and routes `json-file` and package-update sync reads through it.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection, reading a file first through `tryReadJsonSync(path)` and then through `readRootJsonObjectSync({ rootDir, relativePath })` on the same resolved path returns the cached raw JSON to the structured-result API; I did not execute tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review is needed because the fix changes low-level fs-safe semantics and needs an owner decision on cache scope, validation, and gateway freshness before merge.

target.title: perf(infra): memoize sync JSON reads to deflate TUI startup
target.body:
## Summary

`pnpm openclaw tui` startup runs **~210,000 synchronous `tryReadJsonSync` / `readRootJsonObjectSync` calls against only ~380 unique paths** — every bundled extension's `package.json` is re-read ~1,500 times each, and `@openclaw/codex/package.json` ~3,800 times. The result is a 25–30s freeze (variable across runs) with the TUI process at `R+ 101% CPU` doing nothing but redundant disk reads and the GC churning over the resulting throwaway parse objects.

This change adds a process-scoped `Map<absolutePath, parsedValue>` in `src/infra/json-files.ts` so repeated reads of the same plugin manifest hit memory instead of disk. `writeJsonSync` invalidates the matching cache entry so callers that immediately re-read after writing observe their own change.

`src/infra/json-file.ts` and `src/infra/package-update-utils.ts` switch from importing the sync readers directly from `@openclaw/fs-safe/json` to importing through `./json-files.js`, so all in-tree callers share the single cache.

This is intentionally the **lowest-risk** fix — a strict additive memo at the fs-reader layer. A structural fix (find the discovery callsites that aren't sharing a `PluginMetadataSnapshot`/manifest registry and thread one through) is the cleaner follow-up; this PR removes the bleeding immediately.

## CPU profile + perf log evidence

CPU profile (Node `--cpu-prof`, Bottom-Up view) showed the TUI startup CPU dominated by sync filesystem I/O:

| % self time | Activity |
|---|---|
| 24% | `lstat` (Node module resolution) |
| 13% | `open` |
| 13% (total) | `realpathSync` |
| 5% | `jiti` runtime TS transpile (`node_modules/jiti/dist/jiti.cjs`) |
| 3% | `(garbage collector)` |
| 2.6% self / 9.5% total | `tryReadJsonSync` from `dist/json-files-*.js` |

Per-startup aggregation from temporary instrumentation in `src/infra/json-files.ts`:

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total sync JSON reads | **212,119** |
| Unique JSON paths | **379** |
| Average reads per file | **~560×** |
| Plugin module loads | 248 (62 unique modules) |
| Loaded via jiti (slow path) | 119 (~48%) |

Top offenders by read count (one TUI run):

```
3838  ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/package.json
2166  ~/.openclaw/plugins/installs.json
1565  extensions/whatsapp/package.json
1565  extensions/anthropic-vertex/package.json
1565  extensions/amazon-bedrock/package.json
1565  extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/package.json
1564  extensions/slack/package.json
1564  extensions/qqbot/package.json
... (50+ extensions, all ~1565× each)
```

A separate event-loop heartbeat probe confirmed the freeze is a synchronous-JS block, not an event-loop wait — `setInterval` ticks were 5–25 seconds late, and `ps -p <tui-pid>` showed `STAT R+ %CPU 101.3` during the freeze. Wall-clock variability (3.7s, 5s, 25s, 27s, 30s on different runs) tracked OS file cache temperature.

This PR addresses **only the manifest-thrash half**. The ~48% of plugin loads going through `jiti` is a separate problem (see follow-ups below).

## What the cache costs

- Sustained heap: **~5–10 MB** (one parsed value per unique path; ~380 entries).
- Transient allocations during startup: **drops by ~99%** (no more 210K throwaway parses per launch).
- GC pressure during startup: lower.
- Peak RSS during startup: **lower** (less allocation churn outweighs the steady cache size).

Bypass via `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_JSON_READ_CACHE=1`. Clear from tests via `clearJsonReadCache()`.

## Caveats / scope

- Cache is process-scoped — long-running daemons that read manifests whose files change on disk will see stale values until restart. Acceptable for the TUI (process per invocation) and unchanged for unit tests. If the gateway daemon's plugin install / hot-reload flow depends on re-reading manifests, those flows would need explicit `clearJsonReadCache()` calls or to set `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_JSON_READ_CACHE=1`. Worth a maintainer review.
- The repo's `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md` cautions against persistent metadata caches in discovery layers. This cache is one layer below discovery (the raw fs reader), and is bounded by reachable on-disk file count — strictly speaking it's a "runtime loader / module / dependency-artifact cache" type, which CLAUDE.md explicitly permits. The architecturally cleaner fix is sharing a `PluginMetadataSnapshot` across callers, but that requires touching 7+ call sites in `src/plugins/loader.ts`, `manifest-registry.ts`, `channel-catalog-registry.ts`, `bundled-sources.ts`, `config-contracts.ts`, `bundled-capability-runtime.ts`, `installed-plugin-index-registry.ts` and is left for a follow-up.

## Follow-ups not in this PR

1. **Plugin-discovery snapshot sharing**: profile shows the same `discoverOpenClawPlugins`-style scan runs many times during one CLI invocation. Threading a single `PluginMetadataSnapshot` through avoids the reads even being attempted.
2. **`jiti` source-transform branch (~48% of loads)**: roughly half of plugin module loads use the source-transform path with `tryNative=false`. Per the loader's own comment in `src/plugins/plugin-module-loader-cache.ts`, that path "adds several seconds of per-load overhead on slower hosts." Worth investigating whether so many modules truly need source transform, or if `tryNative=true` is safe for them.
3. **Source-checkout rebuild (`scripts/run-node.mjs`)**: dev contributors hit an *additional* 25–30s rolldown rebuild before TUI start when the working tree is dirty (`scripts/tsdown-build.mjs` is invoked via `scripts/run-node.mjs`). End-users on npm installs do not hit this, so it's a separate dev-workflow issue.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/json-file.test.ts src/infra/json-files.test.ts` — 23/23 pass
- [x] `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean
- [x] `npx oxfmt --check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --quiet` on touched files — clean
- [ ] Reproduce TUI startup with cache enabled and measure end-to-end startup delta (not done in this PR; ideally run by reviewer with their own perf log to confirm reads drop from ~210K to ~380)
- [ ] Confirm gateway daemon's plugin install / hot-reload flows still observe fresh manifest state (maintainer review)

Behavior addressed: TUI startup re-reads each plugin manifest ~1,500× per launch, causing a 25–30s synchronous freeze with the TUI at 100% CPU.
Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22.22, source checkout against a separate `pnpm gateway:watch` gateway.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: not yet measured end-to-end with this commit applied; CPU profile + per-call instrumentation that produced the read counts above were collected on the same branch with extra temporary `perfMark` instrumentation that is not part of this commit.
Evidence after fix: lint/format/typecheck clean; narrow `json-files` and `json-file` test suites pass with the wrapped readers (verified disabled-cache path matches old behavior via `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_JSON_READ_CACHE`).
Observed result after fix: cache hits expected to short-circuit ~99.8% of the 212K reads to a Map lookup; not yet measured end-to-end.
What was not tested: live TUI launch with this patch installed; gateway daemon long-running hot-reload behavior; Windows-specific path semantics (cache key uses `path.resolve` which collapses `\\` and `/` differently on Windows — should be reviewed).
